description: Audit (not fix) the Claude Code status line per ADR-2604151500. Deliverable: docs/analysis/statusline-audit-2604151500.md containing per-segment provenance, glyph legend, inaccuracy log, confusion log, and ranked recommendation set. NO code changes in this workplan — every recommendation routes to a downstream ADR draft if action is warranted.

tasks.title: Read hex-cli/assets/helpers/hex-statusline.cjs end-to-end. Enumerate every parts.push(...) call. For each, capture: (1) the segment template, (2) the data variables it consumes, (3) the file:line, (4) the source(s) those variables were populated from (REST endpoint, file, env). Write the table into docs/analysis/statusline-audit-2604151500.md under section ## Provenance Map.

For every data source named in P1.1 that is also exposed as a hex command (e.g. /api/brain/status ↔ `hex sched status`), name the canonical CLI command in a 'Canonical Command' column. Mark segments with NO canonical command — those are status-line-only views with no other way to verify the number.

Extract every Unicode glyph used in the status line via a regex over hex-statusline.cjs. For each, document: (a) symbol, (b) intended meaning, (c) sections it appears in, (d) whether meaning is consistent across sections. Write into the report as ## Glyph Legend with an explicit 'Ambiguity' column flagging glyphs whose meaning shifts by section (initial suspects: ○ used for both 'idle but fine' and 'service down').

For each row in the provenance map with a Canonical Command, run a comparison: capture the status-line value, run the canonical command, diff the numbers. Repeat with the daemon idle and with the daemon mid-drain. Record every disagreement (status, fixture, expected, actual, severity) under ## Inaccuracy Log. Severity rubric: HIGH = wrong sign or order-of-magnitude; MED = stale (value lags by > 1 tick interval); LOW = field-mapping mismatch (e.g. status line shows total, command shows pending).

Specifically verify the four open suspicions raised in ADR-2604151500: (a) brain/sched stale terminology in the live binary; (b) per-project leak — does the line aggregate counts across projects or scope to cwd?; (c) agent_count semantics — live or registered?; (d) ○db visual silence on a REQUIRED dependency. Each suspicion confirmed or refuted in the log with exact reproduction commands.
evidence: grep -n 'brain/sched stale\|per-project leak\|agent_count semantics\|.db visual silence' docs/analysis/statusline-audit-2604151500.md

tasks.title: Enumerate every UX issue found while reading the live line: glyph density (chars/glyph ratio), unlabeled counts, truncation artifacts that look like names, dim-glyph collisions where 'down' and 'idle' use the same color, segment ordering that buries the most operationally important info. Each issue gets a row under ## Confusion Log with a one-sentence proposed mitigation. NO implementation — mitigation column is suggestion only.

Aggregate findings from P3 (Inaccuracy Log) + P4 (Confusion Log) into a single ranked recommendation table under ## Recommendations. Rank rubric: (1) Accuracy impact (HIGH inaccuracy = top), (2) Confusion impact, (3) Implementation cost. For each top-N recommendation (N = top 5 by rank), open a draft ADR stub under docs/adrs/drafts/ADR-statusline-fix-NNN.md with a 1-paragraph problem statement copied from the report, status: Draft. NO implementation in this workplan.

Cross-link the audit report from MEMORY.md under the Active section so future sessions can pick up the recommendations rather than re-investigating. One-line entry only — full content stays in the audit file.
